channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: “I think maybe we will be shaking a bit” – Yandex watson on Crowd at ESL One Birmingham
item.content:encoded:
Alimzhan ‘watson Islambekov is the spearhead of Yandex’s meteoric Dota 2 rise that has already secured them two Tier 1 trophies in the 2025/26 season.

Insider Gaming spoke to watson about the 7.41 patch, Yandex’s run at ESL One Birmingham, and what the team learned from Dmitry ‘DM’ Dorokhin’s successful stand-in showing at PGL Wallachia Season 7.

What’s your first feeling on the patch?

I don’t know, it felt kind of weird. Like we are playing. We played, I think last game in our group, and it’s a new patch, so we just read it all night and then just play.

I like some updates, but not in tournaments.

We saw you play Beastmaster in the Upper Bracket Semi Final. In terms of your own heroes, what are you kind of feeling? Do you feel like they’re in a good place, or do you feel like a lot of them got nerfed?

Some heroes got buffed. I played Beast because it was a good Beast game, but Beast got a bit nerfed, but I also played TB (Terrorblade), Ursa; Ursa is like fully the same, they didn’t touch Ursa.

But they buffed TB a bit, so I played TB, and I’m still trying to find some new heroes on carry.

Okay, so you know obviously next year you’re going to play against Tundra. How are you feeling about Tundra?

They are strong, but we are also not weak, so I think it will be an interesting match.

So speaking of like your own strength, I feel like you’re one of the best in the world, mechanically. Which players would you say stand out to you in terms of mechanics?

I think Satanic is pressing buttons very good. So yeah, Satanic.

You’ve stepped up very quickly to winning Tier 1 events. What kind of principles within the team make it so that you can make such rapid progress?

We have our discipline, structure, how we watch replays, how we prepare for games, how we like even we play pub before our officials.

Everyone should play pubs and we always try hard in pubs, so we don’t have these negative feelings after pubs, and then we play officials with a fresh head.

So you feel like taking pubs seriously has helped you focus on the actual games?

I mean we talked, I remember we had bootcamp before we won DreamLeague. We didn’t like try hard on pubs, everyone was screaming, and we started to play bad.

So then we made a rule that everyone should at least, maybe not full try hard, but at least not screaming in pubs, like not like, oh f**k this guy…

It can be hard to not tilt sometimes in pubs, right?

Yeah.

You’re playing with your full roster. Would it be bad for your chances at TI if you did not play a lot of events this year with your full roster?

It will be very hard, I think, if you miss one player. Every player is important in our team.

You won PGL Wallachia Season 7 with DM as stand-in. Is there anything you learned from DM?

From me, I’m not sure, because I played my game at Wallachia and I’m playing my game here.

Maybe the coach said to notice some maybe things about DM, what he did in-game, so the game was a bit easier, because DM was more experienced.

What kind of things do you think the coach picked up on from DM?

He was talking a lot and he was trying to connect very early. He’s not that greedy in game, so like in Wallachia, me and CHIRA had more space.

And DM was talking a lot, because I’m not talking before minute, I don’t know, 15 something like this.

DM was calling like non-stop early game, so yeah, maybe this.

Playing in front of the crowd, is that something you think is going to be good for the team?

I don’t know, but I think maybe we will be shaking a bit, so yeah, we will just try hard.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: New Rumored Xbox Game Pass Tier Includes Only First Party Games
item.content:encoded:
A new Xbox Game Pass tier could be on the way. And this tier could be different than anything Microsoft has offered to subscribers of the service before.

First spotted by Twitter user redphx and reported by Windows Central, this new possible tier has the codename “Trion”. What makes it different than others, however, is the games being offered.

According to the data Redphx found, it appears to only include first-party games from Xbox Studios. The games that were found through the data mine include:

  • DOOM Eternal
  • Dishonored 2
  • Fable Anniversary
  • Fallout 4
  • Fallout 76
  • Gears 5
  • Halo 5
  • Halo Wars 2
  • Hellblade
  • Ori and the Blind Forest
  • State of Decay 2
  • The Elder Scrolls Online

New Xbox Game Pass Tiers Seem Likely

Hearing about new tiers for Xbox Game Pass shouldn’t be much of a surprise. Earlier this week, rumors started to circulate that Xbox was considering re-introducing lower-priced options for players. There are also rumors about an ad-based tier being added for Xbox Cloud Gaming.

Under new CEO Asha Sharma, the goal for Xbox is to make “future consoles and products like Game Pass more enticing” to more players. How Xbox actually achieves this, however, remains to be seen.

As with any rumor, until it’s officially announced or verified by Insider Gaming, please take everything with a grain of salt. Still, it seems more likely than not that some major changes are coming to Xbox in the near not-so-distant future. The question then becomes, will it be enough to turn things around?

What do you think of more tier options for Xbox Game Pass? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about Sony announcing major price increases for its PlayStation hardware. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Are These The T1 2025 World Championship Skins?
item.content:encoded:
Last year, T1 and Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok won a record third consecutive League of Legends World Championship (LoL Worlds) trophy, meaning the prestigious organisation will get even more iconic in-game skins.

Ever since that incredible triumph in a five-game series against KT Rolster, rumours have been running rampant over who T1 would pick for their next series of skins.

The release will contain Faker’s 11th skin as well as a first Worlds skin for toplaner Choi ‘Doran’ Hyeon-joon, and third LoL skins for jungler Mun ‘Oner’ Hyeon-jun, botlaner Lee ‘Gumayusi’ Min-hyeong, and support Ryu ‘Keria’ Min-seo

And now, according to Namu wiki, most of their champion choices have been locked in, with the skins expected to release in either August or September later this year.

What Champions Have T1 Picked For Their LoL Worlds 2025 skins?

According to the wiki site, the three locked-in T1 skins are:

  • Doran – Ambessa
  • Oner – Xin Zhao
  • Faker – Galio

Namu wiki also claims that Im ‘Tom’ Jae-hyeon, who is stepping in for kkOma as T1 coach, will be receiving an in-game ward skin.

Ambessa is Doran’s signature champion and was always his most likely choice.

Oner played 30 games of Xin Zhao in 2025, almost double that of any other champion, and yet maintained a 76.7% win rate with the reworked character.

As for Faker, Mel had been a rumoured choice and then Taliyah was reported as a possible option. However, it now appears that he will select Galio, a champion he almost picked in the past.

Another change has reportedly be made by Keria as well, who was previously rumoured to pick Neeko but now has apparently chosen Seraphine.

Meanwhile Gumayusi, who now plays for Hanwha Life Esports, is seemingly set to stick with Yunara as his first pick and Miss Fortune for his prestige skin. After being awarded the MVP award for the LoL Worlds final, the Korean ADC was entitled to a second skin.

Which T1 Worlds skin are you most excited for? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see what changes Riot has made to LoL MSI and Worlds this year.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Should You Upgrade To A PS5 Pro In 2026?
item.content:encoded:
Picking the right time to upgrade your console is a serious choice to make, and in this economic climate, it’s not one that comes easily. It used to be that you could comfortably upgrade your console every generation, but as time has gone by, it’s becoming more and more common to make the jump mid-generation instead.

It’s exactly the same case with the PlayStation 5, and knowing whether to make the jump to the PS5 Pro, especially with the latest round of price increases. It’s an incredibly difficult decision to make, but luckily for you, Insider Gaming is here to help you settle the score.

Here are the differences between the standard PS5 and PS5 Pro, as well as whether it’s worth picking up the upgrade.

What Are The Differences Between The PS5 & PS5 Pro?

The biggest gap between the PS5 and PS5 Pro comes down to performance. The Pro model is designed to deliver higher frame rates alongside noticeable visual upgrades, making it the most powerful console currently available.

A major factor behind this leap is improved ray tracing performance and the introduction of PSSR (PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution). This AI-driven upscaling technology enhances image quality while maintaining smoother performance, allowing games to look sharper without sacrificing frame rate. It’s a feature exclusive to the PS5 Pro and one that significantly elevates its overall output.

  • More Insider Gaming: When Do PS5, PS5 Pro & PlayStation Price Increases Happen In 2026?

Here’s how the two consoles compare on paper:

In short, the PS5 Pro offers a sizeable boost in raw power, double the storage, and access to advanced upscaling tech that simply isn’t available on the standard model.

It’s all well and good being a stronger console; that much is obvious, but is it worth the price? Because a console this strong doesn’t come cheap.

What Is The Price Difference Between The PS5 & PS5 Pro?

The PS5 Pro is bound to be a hot topic due to Sony’s recent price increases to all PS5 hardware, with the PS5 Pro suffering the most.

Following recent price increases, the PS5 Pro now sits at around $899, marking a $150 jump from its original pricing. Meanwhile, the standard PS5 comes in at $649, leaving a significant $250 gap between the two.

That price difference is hard to ignore. For many players, that extra spend could go towards additional accessories, games, or even another piece of hardware entirely (such as the freshly-priced PlayStation Portal). When you factor in ongoing costs like PS Plus subscriptions and new releases, the Pro quickly becomes a serious financial commitment.

It’s also worth noting that the PS5 Pro is entirely digital—unless you buy the separate disc drive. So, if you’re a collector or believer in physical media, you’re going to have to cough up even more money to buy into the cause.

Should You Upgrade To The PS5 Pro?

Ultimately, whether you upgrade comes down to how much you value performance—and how much you’re willing to spend to get it.

There’s no denying that the PS5 Pro is the superior console. It offers better frame rates, improved visuals, and a level of future-proofing that the standard PS5 can’t quite match. If you want the best possible console experience over the next few years, it’s the clear choice.

Timing also plays a role. With hardware prices trending upwards, waiting could mean paying even more down the line. If you’re already considering the upgrade, there’s a strong argument for making the jump sooner rather than later.

  • More Insider Gaming: Every PlayStation Studio That’s Closed Since The PS5 Launched

Then there’s the games. Upcoming heavy hitters like Grand Theft Auto 6 are expected to push current hardware to its limits, and rumours suggest that the PS5 Pro may be the only console capable of delivering a stable 60fps experience. The same applies to demanding titles like Crimson Desert, which has already highlighted performance gaps between the two systems.

That said, the standard PS5 is still a very capable machine. If you’re happy with current performance and don’t feel the need to chase cutting-edge visuals, sticking with what you have remains a perfectly valid choice.

In the end, the PS5 Pro isn’t essential, but if you want the best version of console gaming available right now, it’s hard to beat.

Will you be buying a PS5 Pro? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming PlayStation coverage, check out the dynamic prices that offer wildly different discounts for some games.
